title: Accessible solar tower power plant data to accelerate solar energy research - Nature Energy
text:
Over 800 GB of high-resolution operational solar tower power plant data have been compiled to form an open-access interface that complies with FAIR data principles. This database, known as PAINT, democratizes access to structured operational data, with the goal of facilitating the development of data-driven solutions in solar tower research.

ReferencesAlexopoulos, S. & Hoffschmidt, B. Advances in solar tower technology. Wiley Interdiscip. Rev. Energy Environ. 6, e217 (2017). This review article provides an intuitive explanation of solar tower power plants and an overview of open challenges.
Google Scholar 
Zhu, G. et al. HelioCon: a roadmap for advanced heliostat technologies for concentrating solar power. Sol. Energy 264, 111917 (2023). This article outlines development pathways for concentrating solar power technologies, highlighting the importance of open data.Article 

Google Scholar 
Wilkinson, M. D. et al. The FAIR Guiding Principles for scientific data management and stewardship. Sci. Data 3, 160018 (2016). An overview of the FAIR data principles.Article 

Google Scholar 
Pargmann, M. et al. Automatic heliostat learning for in situ concentrating solar power plant metrology with differentiable ray tracing. Nat. Commun. 15, 6997 (2024). This article highlights the use of data-driven models to improve heliostat flux prediction and surface characterization.Article 

Google Scholar 
Busch, M. et al. ARTIST - AI-enhanced differentiable Ray Tracer for Irradiation Prediction in Solar Tower Digital Twins. GitHub https://github.com/ARTIST-Association/ARTIST (2026).
